questions.question: What best describes the problem of scarcity? A All consumer wants must be satisfied., B All resources are free at the point of use., C There are limited resources and unlimited wants., D There are unlimited resources and limited wants.
questions.text: What best describes the problem of scarcity? A All consumer wants must be satisfied., B All resources are free at the point of use., C There are limited resources and unlimited wants., D There are unlimited resources and limited wants.
options: A All consumer wants must be satisfied.
B All resources are free at the point of use.
C There are limited resources and unlimited wants.
D There are unlimited resources and limited wants.

questions.question: What is an example of the purchase of a capital good? A a farm owner purchasing additional land, B a farm owner purchasing a television, C a farm owner purchasing a tractor, D a farm worker purchasing some seeds
questions.text: What is an example of the purchase of a capital good? A a farm owner purchasing additional land, B a farm owner purchasing a television, C a farm owner purchasing a tractor, D a farm worker purchasing some seeds
options: A a farm owner purchasing additional land
B a farm owner purchasing a television
C a farm owner purchasing a tractor
D a farm worker purchasing some seeds

questions.question: A student decides to buy a ticket for a concert instead of buying a new shirt. What is the opportunity cost of buying the ticket? A the    concert, B the price of the ticket to the concert, C the    shirt, D the time spent purchasing the ticket
questions.text: A student decides to buy a ticket for a concert instead of buying a new shirt. What is the opportunity cost of buying the ticket? A the    concert, B the price of the ticket to the concert, C the    shirt, D the time spent purchasing the ticket
options: A the    concert
B the price of the ticket to the concert
C the    shirt
D the time spent purchasing the ticket

questions.question: In a market system who has the most important role in influencing the allocation of resources? A consumers, B entrepreneurs, C government    officials, D producers 3
questions.text: In a market system who has the most important role in influencing the allocation of resources? A consumers, B entrepreneurs, C government    officials, D producers 3
options: A consumers
B entrepreneurs
C government    officials
D producers 3

questions.question: The diagram shows the market for oil. The initial equilibrium is X. If  the  quantity  of  oil  sold  increased  by  two  million  barrels  a  day,  which  point  represents  the  new equilibrium? price quantity O S 2 D 2 S 1 S 3 D 1 D 3 X A B C D

questions.question: What is an external cost of mining coal? A the cost of purchasing mining equipment, B the extra cleaning costs of local residents’ houses because of coal dust, C the extra cleaning costs of the mine buildings because of coal dust, D the cost to the mine owners to remove waste
questions.text: What is an external cost of mining coal? A the cost of purchasing mining equipment, B the extra cleaning costs of local residents’ houses because of coal dust, C the extra cleaning costs of the mine buildings because of coal dust, D the cost to the mine owners to remove waste

B the extra cleaning costs of local residents’ houses because of coal dust

D the cost to the mine owners to remove waste

questions.question: The table shows the demand for chocolates at two different prices. price $ quantity demanded 0.50                 400 0.45                 480 What is the price elasticity of demand when the price falls from $0.50 to $0.45? A less than 0.5, B between 0.5 and 1, C between 1 and 1.5, D greater than 1.5 4

questions.question: What is a direct benefit to a worker of specialisation? A the higher output that may lead to higher earnings, B the higher output that may lead to higher profits, C the higher output that may lead to lower costs per unit produced, D the longer training period that is required before starting work

questions.question: The table shows some of the costs of running a retail shop in one week. $ rent of shop 200 insurance premium 40 purchase of stock 700 delivery of goods 50 What are the total fixed costs during this week? A $200    B    $240    C    $700    D    $750

questions.question: A company’s annual report suggested that it had benefited from some changes. Which change is an internal economy of scale for this company? A an improvement in road and rail facilities that has reduced its distribution costs, B an increase in the number of local specialist workers that it could recruit, C the introduction of new training at a local college for which staff have enrolled, D the reduction in the company’s costs by the purchase of a factory supplying components

options: A an improvement in road and rail facilities that has reduced its distribution costs
B an increase in the number of local specialist workers that it could recruit
C the introduction of new training at a local college for which staff have enrolled
D the reduction in the company’s costs by the purchase of a factory supplying components

questions.question: A government believes that consumption of a certain good will be of benefit to society. Which policy is most likely to encourage producers to increase their output of that good? A an increase in the period required for training the industry’s workers, B an increase in the regulations affecting businesses, C an increase in the subsidies for that good, D an increase in the taxation of that good 6

questions.question: Which statement describes a progressive tax? A a tax that has a higher percentage rate as income rises, B a tax that increases government income over time, C a tax that places an increasing burden on the poor, D a tax that rises in line with the rate of inflation

questions.question: Which combination of government policies is most likely to reduce unemployment? income tax rate government spending rate of interest A decrease             decrease              increase, B decrease              increase              decrease, C increase              decrease              decrease, D increase              increase              increase
questions.text: Which combination of government policies is most likely to reduce unemployment? income tax rate government spending rate of interest A decrease             decrease              increase, B decrease              increase              decrease, C increase              decrease              decrease, D increase              increase              increase

questions.question: Which pair of government actions combines a fiscal policy with a supply-side policy? A changing government spending with changing interest rates, B cutting tax rates with providing information on job vacancies, C limiting bank lending with the privatisation of industry, D restricting credit with less control over labour markets

B cutting tax rates with providing information on job vacancies

D restricting credit with less control over labour markets

questions.question: What is a recession? A a period when incomes rise by less than inflation, B a period when prices are falling continuously for twelve months, C a period when real GDP is declining for two consecutive quarters, D a period when unemployment is rising
questions.text: What is a recession? A a period when incomes rise by less than inflation, B a period when prices are falling continuously for twelve months, C a period when real GDP is declining for two consecutive quarters, D a period when unemployment is rising

B a period when prices are falling continuously for twelve months

questions.question: An economy is suffering from a period of deflation. What does this mean for the economy? A Consumers may delay purchase of some products., B Purchasing power of consumers falls., C Tax revenues for the government will rise., D The real value of personal savings falls. 7

questions.question: Official  statistics  in  2010  showed  South  Africa  had  a  very  high  unemployment  rate  at  around 25%, although wages were double those in Brazil. What might be a reason for South Africa’s high wages in a time of high unemployment compared with Brazil? A a skilled workforce in Brazil, B high levels of savings in South Africa, C more labour-intensive production in Brazil, D strong trade unions in South Africa

B high levels of savings in South Africa

questions.question: What would cause a favourable change in the Indian trade in services (invisible) account? A A Japanese company invests in a subsidiary in India., B A large Indian company increases its exports of goods., C A Malaysian company increases its exports to India., D An Indian company wins a contract to transport South African exports. 9

questions.question: In  October  2015,  one  UK  pound  could  be  exchanged  for  100  Indian  rupees.  By  October  2016, one UK pound could be exchanged for 80 Indian rupees. What would be a short-term consequence of this change? A Economic growth in India would increase., B Indian manufacturers would earn higher profits from exports to the UK., C Inflationary pressure in the UK would reduce., D UK manufacturers would find it easier to export to India.
questions.text: In  October  2015,  one  UK  pound  could  be  exchanged  for  100  Indian  rupees.  By  October  2016, one UK pound could be exchanged for 80 Indian rupees. What would be a short-term consequence of this change? A Economic growth in India would increase., B Indian manufacturers would earn higher profits from exports to the UK., C Inflationary pressure in the UK would reduce., D UK manufacturers would find it easier to export to India.
options: A Economic growth in India would increase.
B Indian manufacturers would earn higher profits from exports to the UK.
C Inflationary pressure in the UK would reduce.
D UK manufacturers would find it easier to export to India.
